skill.name: longbridge-derivatives
skill.description: Use Longbridge market data to inspect options chains, quotes, volume, Greeks, implied volatility, strategy candidates, P&L snapshots, and Hong Kong warrants.
skill.summary: Analyze options, implied volatility, Greeks, strategies, P&L, and HK warrants with Longbridge data.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and environment-access patterns. Review found the backtick matches are Markdown inline code or bash examples for the Longbridge CLI, while the high-severity crypto and reconnaissance matches are false positives from trading documentation and templates. The skill is publishable with a warning because it intentionally directs agents to run external Longbridge CLI commands with market symbols supplied by users.

medium_findings.title: External Longbridge CLI Commands With User Market Inputs
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Longbridge CLI commands for option chains, quotes, volume, kline data, warrants, and strategy analysis. This is legitimate read-oriented market-data behavior, but it is still external command execution using user-provided symbols, dates, and contracts, so callers should pass arguments safely and avoid shell interpolation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain direct Longbridge CLI command instructions and examples. The commands match the skill purpose and do not show malicious intent, but the external command behavior is intentional and user-parameterized.

low_findings.title: Static Ruby Backtick Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer labeled many inline backtick spans and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations are Markdown documentation for command names, placeholders, and output templates, not executable Ruby code or command-substitution syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown references and the marked backticks wrap command names, placeholders, and examples. No Ruby source file or backtick command-substitution expression was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Matches Are Domain-Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The high and low blocker matches occur in ordinary options strategy text and output templates, including phrases about risk, strategy alternatives, liquidity, and volatility percentiles. No cryptographic API, hashing call, host reconnaissance command, or system inventory behavior is present in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the marked lines shows finance terminology and report-template text only. There is no code path, cryptographic primitive, or system command at the cited lines.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Match Is Not Process Environment Access
low_findings.description: The environment-access match points to options strategy workflow text, not code that reads environment variables. No process.env, shell variable expansion, credential loading, or configuration-file access is present at the cited location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line asks the assistant to clarify a user market view and time horizon. It does not access environment variables or secrets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parameterized External CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: Several references show commands that substitute user-supplied market symbols, dates, expiries, and option identifiers into Longbridge CLI calls. This is expected for the skill, but integrations should pass arguments as structured argv values and reject shell metacharacters in market identifiers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples clearly contain user-facing placeholders such as symbols, dates, and OCC identifiers. The risk depends on the host agent execution method, so the pattern needs integration-level controls rather than blocking the skill.

content.value_statement: Derivatives research often requires chains, Greeks, volatility, and payoff context from several commands. This skill organizes Longbridge option and warrant data into structured analysis for quotes, strategies, P&L, and volatility review.
actual_capabilities: Looks up option chains, expiries, strikes, OCC symbols, quotes, volume, and open interest through Longbridge commands.
Summarizes option Greeks, including delta, gamma, theta, and vega, when quote data is available.
Compares implied volatility with historical volatility and describes smile, skew, and percentile signals.
Explains common option strategies, including spreads, straddles, strangles, covered calls, and protective puts.
Builds P&L snapshots with breakeven, max profit, max loss, and net Greeks for single-leg or multi-leg positions.
Finds Hong Kong warrants, callable bull or bear contracts, and issuer lists supported by Longbridge.
limitations: It depends on the Longbridge CLI or matching MCP tools being available in the user environment.
It does not place trades or provide personalized investment advice.
Some commands may require market access, available data, or an authenticated Longbridge setup.
Strategy metrics can be approximate when liquidity, Greeks, or historical data are incomplete.

use_cases.title: Review an Option Chain
use_cases.description: Inspect expiries, strikes, call and put symbols, volume, open interest, and available Greeks before comparing contracts.

use_cases.title: Build a Strategy Brief
use_cases.description: Match a bullish, bearish, neutral, or volatility view to strategy candidates and estimated risk metrics.

use_cases.title: Analyze Volatility Signals
use_cases.description: Compare implied volatility, historical volatility, skew, and term structure to explain current derivatives pricing.

prompt_templates.title: Find Available Contracts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge to list available option expiries and nearby strikes for TSLA.US. Summarize call and put choices in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Explain One Contract
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this option contract with Longbridge data: AAPL240119C190000. Explain price, IV, volume, open interest, and Greeks.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Strategy Candidates
prompt_templates.prompt: I am moderately bullish on 700.HK for the next month. Compare two option or warrant strategies and explain risk, reward, and data limits.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Volatility Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge data to compare near and far expiry IV, 60-day historical volatility, and skew for NVDA.US. Suggest an educational strategy framework.

output_examples.input: Show the option chain for AAPL.US and explain the nearest expiry.
output_examples.output: A concise chain summary with available expiries, nearby strikes, call and put symbols, liquidity notes, and the next question needed to narrow the contract.

output_examples.input: Compare a long call and bull call spread for TSLA.US.
output_examples.output: A strategy comparison that lists structure, estimated cost, breakeven, max loss, upside profile, IV context, and key risks.

output_examples.input: Find HK warrants for 700.HK and explain issuer differences.
output_examples.output: A warrant overview with contract type, issuer, leverage, premium, implied volatility, maturity, and a plain-language risk note.

best_practices: Provide the market suffix, such as .US or .HK, when asking for a symbol lookup.
Give expiry, strike, and call or put direction when you already know the contract you want.
Ask for risk, liquidity, and data-quality notes before comparing option or warrant choices.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the analysis as a trade order or personalized financial advice.
Do not compare stale quotes without checking the timestamp and market session.
Do not use vague symbols when multiple markets or underlying securities could match.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It retrieves and explains market data. It does not submit orders or manage positions.

faq.question: Which markets does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on US options and Hong Kong warrants or callable bull and bear contracts supported by Longbridge.

faq.question: Can it calculate option Greeks?
faq.answer: It can report Greeks from Longbridge quote data and summarize net Greeks for multi-leg positions when inputs are available.

faq.question: Can it analyze implied volatility?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare IV, historical volatility, volatility skew, smile shape, and term structure using available data.

faq.question: What should I include in a prompt?
faq.answer: Include symbol, market, expiry window, strike range, strategy view, and whether you want quotes, P&L, or volatility analysis.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to run Longbridge CLI commands. This is expected, but host tools should pass user inputs safely.
